views.title: How a thermostat holds a temperature
views.summary: A sensor, a comparison, and a switch, repeated forever. The room itself closes the loop: heat changes the very reading that decides whether to heat.

steps.title: The room is measured
steps.detail: The sensor samples the air every few seconds. Everything downstream trusts this single number.

steps.title: Is the room too cold?
steps.detail: The controller subtracts the reading from the target. A small dead band around the target stops the heater from flapping on and off.

branches.when: below target minus the dead band
inside or above the dead band

steps.title: The heater switches on
steps.detail: Heat flows into the room, which slowly raises the next sensor reading; the action changes the input that triggered it.

steps.title: Everything rests
steps.detail: The heater stays off while the room slowly leaks heat to the outside, drifting the reading back down until the cycle repeats.

loops.label: heat leaking away drags the reading back down
